Patient: LI LORE, JENNI FER M
Jennifer M Liloreis a 48 year old female with PMH of HTN, asthma, seizure disorder, chronic pain
syndrome with yneur and spinal cord stimulator, depressio who was admitted to the
energency department from skilled nursing facility on 03/30/2026 after she was last seen well
at 4 PM and found unresponsive at 6 PM Emergency medi services brought her to the hospital,
where initial workup reveal a hemogl obin of 10.5, serumsodi um of 145, and a urine ogy
screen positive for benzodi azepines. She was extremely somol and only arousable to sternal rub,
requiring Narcan administration both at the skilled nursing facility and again by EMS en route to
the hospital. Her hospital course was complicated by persistent altered ment al status, garbled
speech, repetitive words, twitching and jerking movements, suspected aspiration with acute hypoxia,
and a negative EEG f or seizure activity. Imaging studies including CT head without contrast and CT
chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast showed no acute intracranial or cardi opul nonar y
pathology. Sherequired bilateral wist restraints and lower extremity splints for safety. a not e,
she al so has mpaired mobility due to a recent fall and right lower extremity injury, which is
inmobilized in a cast. Patient is now admitted to Encompass acute inpatient rehab for comprehensive
therapy program prior to safe discharge home.
Today, patient seen sitting up confortably in bed. She had ortho follow up appointment this morning
for her cast removal (now CAM boot). She reports current pain level 9.5/10 though objectively
appears confortable during discussion. She has not used her prn audi and pain currently managed
with prn tylenol and schedul gabapentin. She does typically take bacl of 20 mg tid at home,
though she states she takes inconsistently. Discussed concern with bacl of withdrawal with sudden
cessation, as well as possible lowering of seizure threshold. She is agreeable to resume at lower
dose. No reported fevers, chills, chest pain, or shortness of breath.

Patient lives alone in a one-story home with no steps to enter. Prior to admission, patient was
ndependent with ambulation, activities of daily living, and cognition. Currently, patient requires
supervision for bed mobility and minimal assistance for transfers, anbulation, and basic ADLs,
reflecting a significant decline in ndependence. Patient uses a walker for mobility. Substance use
history, including al cohol , tobacco, and other substances, is unknown due to cognitive
impairment. Patient's prior transportation abilities are not document ed, but current functional
decline and use of a walker suggest limited ability to travel independently.
Functional Assessment (current vs. prior level of function that preceded current medical / surgical
The patient was previously ndependent with anbulation, activities of daily living (ADLs), and
cognition, but now requires supervision for bed mobility and minimal assistance for transfers,
ambulation, and ADLs, reflecting a significant decline in functional status. This decline is
associated with acute toxic-metabolio encephal opat hy, recent fall with right lower extremity injury,
and increased pain and fall risk.

PT: ROM and stretching exercises. Bed Mobility, Transfers, Static/dynamic Bal ance, Anbulati on with
least restrictive adaptive device, Home management, Family Training. HEP.
Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Las Vegas
OT: BUE strengthening and stretching. ADL/iADL re-training with adaptive equi prent PRN. Safety,
Bal ance with self care, Gross/Fine Motor Movements, Modalities PRN, Splints PRN Home management.
Intensity of Services: [3 hours of therapy a day/ 5 days a week]
Goal s: Modified ndependent for self care, transfers and for ambulation.
Considering all of the information above, it is my best judgment that this patient requires
intensive rehabilitation as described above in an inpatient hospital environment due to the
complexity of nursing, medical management, and rehabilitation needs, and can reasonably be expected
to participate in and benefit from an Inpatient stay with an plinary team appr oach to the
delivery of rehabilitation care under the direction and supervision of a rehabilitation physician.

This patient is currently under my care, and I have initiated the establishment of a plan of care
for home health services following the face-to-face encounter on the date noted above. I certify
that this patient is confined to his/her home and needs intermittent skilled services (nursing care,
physical therapy and/or speech therapy or continues to need occupational therapy) as indicated in
the above summary of impaired body structures that supports the homebound status and need for
skilled care. This patient will be followed by their PCP who will be responsible for further review
of the plan of care and recertification as applicable.
